clean_code:
# Use realistic slippage — 5-10 bps is a common conservative estimate
set_slippage(slippage.FixedBasisPointsSlippage(basis_points=5, volume_limit=0.1))
description: Slippage is explicitly set to zero basis points.
explanation: In real trading, you never execute at exactly the last close price. Market impact, bid-ask spread, and latency all cause slippage. Setting basis_points=0 assumes perfect execution, which inflates backtest performance. Strategies that appear profitable at zero slippage often become unprofitable at even 3-5 basis points.
suggested_fix: Use at least 5 basis points for liquid large-cap stocks. Use 10-30 basis points for smaller or less liquid instruments.
